_meta.description: Bundled baseline of common reference phases for high-pressure (DAC) and general powder-XRD studies: pressure markers, gasket materials, pressure media, geometry calibrants, and common structures. Each entry carries a space group + ambient lattice + asymmetric-unit atoms (so peak positions and relative intensities can be simulated) and, where a reliable literature value exists, an equation of state. EOS forms supported: BM2/BM3/BM4 (Birch-Murnaghan), Vinet, and Murnaghan; type is set per entry. Entries without an EOS are still usable for phase identification at ambient pressure.
_meta.disclaimer: These are reference STARTING values compiled from common literature at ~300 K. Reported EOS parameters vary between studies (and are correlated, especially K0 vs K0'); atom positions for some structures are nominal (peak POSITIONS depend only on the lattice + space group and are reliable; relative INTENSITIES are approximate). Verify the EOS and the scale/protocol you use before quantitative pressure determination. Workspace user entries shadow these by name. An eos 'p_max' (GPa) marks the entry's validity ceiling (usually a phase transition): identification will not search above it and the ML simulators will not render the phase there.

phases.name: Au
phases.source: Anderson, Isaak & Yamamoto (1989), J. Appl. Phys. 65, 1534, doi:10.1063/1.342969 (K0T = 167 GPa, K0' = 5.5).
phases.notes: Classic FCC pressure marker; many alternative scales exist (e.g. Tsuchiya 2003, Fei 2007, Dorogokupets & Dewaele 2007).

phases.name: Pt
phases.source: Fei, Ricolleau, Frank, Mibe, Shen & Prakapenka (2007), Proc. Natl. Acad. Sci. USA 104, 9182, doi:10.1073/pnas.0609013104 (Vinet, K0T = 273 GPa, K0T' = 5.20).
phases.notes: FCC. Stiff and chemically inert; good high-pressure marker. See also Dorogokupets & Dewaele (2007).

phases.name: Cu
phases.source: Dewaele, Loubeyre & Mezouar (2004), Phys. Rev. B 70, 094112.
phases.notes: FCC.

phases.name: Ag
phases.source: Dewaele, Loubeyre & Mezouar (2004), Phys. Rev. B 70, 094112.
phases.notes: FCC.

phases.name: W
phases.source: Dewaele, Loubeyre & Mezouar (2004), Phys. Rev. B 70, 094112.
phases.notes: BCC. Also frequently used as a gasket material.

phases.name: Mo
phases.source: Dewaele, Loubeyre & Mezouar (2004), Phys. Rev. B 70, 094112.
phases.notes: BCC.

phases.name: MgO
phases.source: Speziale, Zha, Duffy, Hemley & Mao (2001), J. Geophys. Res. 106, 515.
phases.notes: Periclase, rocksalt (B1) structure. Common internal standard, stable to very high P.

phases.name: NaCl-B1
phases.source: Brown (1999), J. Appl. Phys. 86, 5801 (Decker-type scale).
phases.notes: Rocksalt (B1) phase. Transforms to the CsCl-type B2 phase near ~30 GPa (room T) — this entry is only valid below the transition. Also widely used as a soft pressure medium.

phases.name: Re
phases.source: Anzellini, Dewaele, Occelli, Loubeyre & Mezouar (2014), J. Appl. Phys. 115, 043511, doi:10.1063/1.4863300 (Vinet, K0 = 352.6 GPa, K0' = 4.56, He medium to 144 GPa).
phases.notes: HCP. The most common DAC gasket; its rings nearly always appear in the pattern, so it is useful both as a contaminant to recognize and as a secondary marker.

phases.name: Ne
phases.source: Dewaele, Datchi, Loubeyre & Mezouar (2008), Phys. Rev. B 77, 094106 (Vinet cold curve).
phases.notes: Solid Ne (FCC) above ~4.8 GPa. Excellent quasi-hydrostatic medium; V0/K0 are EOS-fit extrapolations to zero pressure, not the ambient solid. NOTE: for K0 ~ 1 GPa the EOS FORM matters (BM3 vs Vinet differ ~2-3% in V over 5-15 GPa, ~1% in d) - keep the form matched to the source fit.

phases.name: Ar
phases.source: Ross, Mao, Bell & Xu (1986); Dewaele et al. (2008).
phases.notes: Solid Ar (FCC). Quasi-hydrostatic medium; EOS parameters are zero-pressure extrapolations.

phases.name: He
phases.source: Loubeyre et al. (1993), Phys. Rev. Lett. 71, 2272.
phases.notes: Helium pressure medium (gas at ambient; solidifies ~11.5 GPa). No ambient cold EOS shipped — enter Loubeyre et al. (1993) parameters for the solid if needed.

phases.name: Stainless steel (gasket)
phases.source: Generic contaminant category; composition and structure depend on the stainless-steel grade.
phases.notes: Common gasket alloy (e.g. 301/304). Polycrystalline BCC/FCC mixture with composition-dependent lattice; no single clean EOS is bundled. Listed so its rings can be flagged as a known contaminant; supply a CIF/EOS if you need quantitative treatment.

phases.name: Al
phases.source: Dewaele, Loubeyre & Mezouar (2004), Phys. Rev. B 70, 094112.
phases.notes: FCC aluminium pressure marker (quasi-hydrostatic to 153 GPa).

phases.name: Ta
phases.source: Cynn & Yoo (1999), Phys. Rev. B 59, 8526 (Ta to 174 GPa).
phases.notes: BCC tantalum marker/gasket; soft K0' makes the scale sensitive.

phases.name: Ni
phases.source: Lattice: Wyckoff, Crystal Structures (1963).
phases.notes: FCC nickel. Structure for phase ID; add an EOS (e.g. Dewaele 2008) for pressure.

phases.name: Al2O3 (corundum)
phases.source: EOS: Dewaele & Torrent (2013); structure: corundum, R-3c.
phases.notes: Corundum/sapphire. Trigonal: isotropic scaling is approximate (anisotropic at high P).

phases.name: Diamond (C)
phases.source: Occelli, Loubeyre & LeToullec (2003), Nat. Mater. 2, 151 (K0'=4 fixed).
phases.notes: Diamond (the anvil); appears as single-crystal spots, suppressed in the robust pattern.

phases.name: Si
phases.source: Hu, Merkle, Menoni & Spain (1986), Phys. Rev. B 34, 4679, doi:10.1103/PhysRevB.34.4679; diamond-cubic Si (valid below the ~11 GPa transition).
phases.notes: Diamond-cubic silicon; transforms to beta-Sn near 11 GPa.

phases.name: CeO2 (ceria)
phases.source: Fluorite CeO2, a=5.4113 Å (powder-diffraction calibrant).
phases.notes: Common geometry calibrant; structure only (used at ambient).

phases.name: LaB6
phases.source: NIST SRM 660 line-position standard, a=4.15689 Å.
phases.notes: Standard geometry/line-shape calibrant; structure only (used at ambient).

phases.name: Fe (alpha, bcc)
phases.source: Lattice: alpha-Fe, a=2.8665 Å (ambient).
phases.notes: BCC alpha-iron; transforms to hcp (epsilon) near 13 GPa — add the hcp EOS for high-P work.
